llm_gate_results.reasoning: The implementation faithfully covers all explicit requirements: (1) incorrect answers trigger enqueueRevisit() storing referenceTime/editTarget/refTarget in revisitQueue in the central store; (2) problems are reintroduced after exactly 2 other problems via roundsSinceEnqueue >= REVISIT_AFTER where REVISIT_AFTER=2; (3) max queue length is 5 via REVISIT_QUEUE_MAX=5 with oldest-drop using .slice(1); (4) revisit problems are rendered identically to fresh problems — no visual distinction in main.js rendering; (5) the queue lives in createGameStore() state and is not persisted across sessions. The underlying purpose — helping players practice problems they struggle with, seamlessly integrated into the game flow — is also captured: the problem's original display state is preserved (editTarget, refTarget, referenceTime) while editTime is reset to initial, giving the player a genuine retry experience.

Only store.js was modified; no test files were altered. All existing tests (130+ across 6 files) retain real assertions and meaningful coverage. The implementation added a revisit queue feature without corresponding new tests, but this is a coverage gap rather than test quality degradation — no existing tests were removed, commented out, or weakened to accommodate the changes.
The implementation only modifies store.js with pure logic changes (spaced repetition revisit queue). No UI components, CSS, or HTML were changed, so there are no visual defects to inspect.
